none: Asheville community development plan : environmental impact statement.

The East End/Valley Street Redevelopment Plan in Asheville, North Carolina, aimed to revitalize a historically neglected and deteriorating neighborhood. The plan proposed improving infrastructure, such as streets and utilities, and enhancing community facilities. It also focused on reducing population density by relocating some residents and businesses. The project sought to improve living conditions and aesthetics, ultimately benefiting the entire city by stabilizing the tax base and increasing property values. The plan emphasized community involvement, with residents actively participating in the planning process to ensure the redevelopment met their needs.

The East End/Valley Street Redevelopment Plan in Asheville, North Carolina, aimed to revitalize a historically neglected and deteriorating neighborhood. The plan proposed improving infrastructure, such as streets and utilities, and enhancing community facilities. It also focused on reducing population density by relocating some residents and businesses. The project sought to improve living conditions and aesthetics, ultimately benefiting the entire city by stabilizing the tax base and increasing property values. The plan emphasized community involvement, with residents actively participating in the planning process to ensure the redevelopment met their needs.
East End/Valley Street, Asheville, North Carolina

The Asheville community development plan was proposed to address the long-standing neglect and deterioration of the East End/Valley Street neighborhood, aiming to revitalize the area through infrastructure improvements and enhanced community facilities. There was public involvement in the planning process, which helped to shape the project according to residents' needs. While there may have been some pushback regarding the relocation of residents and businesses, the overall goal was to improve living conditions and stabilize property values in the city. The project ultimately aimed to create a more vibrant and sustainable community.
United States. Department of Housing and Urban Development
United States. Department of Housing and Urban Development

"The East End/Valley Street Community is the most physically deteriorated area of the city. The area has been neglected and received hardly any improvements over the past decade." — East End/Valley Street Improvement Committee
"Let us commit ourselves to work together to make a change. We must reduce and ultimately eliminate the blight of our city." — East End/Valley Street Improvement Committee
